parts.description:
A button within a toolbar.

See https://base-ui.com/react/components/toolbar

inputs.name: focusableWhenDisabled
inputs.description:
Whether the button stays focusable while disabled (so it remains discoverable for keyboard
and screen-reader users).

inputs.name: nativeButton
inputs.description: Whether the host is a native `<button>`. When `false`, adds `role="button"`.

parts.description:
Groups several toolbar items together. Disabling the group disables all of its items.

See https://base-ui.com/react/components/toolbar

A native input within a toolbar. Participates in the toolbar's composite focus while keeping native
text-editing: the arrow keys move the caret within the text and only move focus to the
neighbouring toolbar item once the caret is at the corresponding edge (Base UI "composite" input).

See https://base-ui.com/react/components/toolbar

inputs.name: defaultValue
inputs.description: The initial value of the uncontrolled input.

inputs.name: focusableWhenDisabled
inputs.description: Whether the input stays focusable while disabled.

parts.description:
A link within a toolbar.

See https://base-ui.com/react/components/toolbar

A container for grouping a set of controls, such as buttons, toggle groups or menus.
Owns composite keyboard focus over its items.

See https://base-ui.com/react/components/toolbar

inputs.name: disabled
inputs.description: Whether the whole toolbar is disabled.

inputs.name: loopFocus
inputs.description: Whether keyboard navigation should loop from the last item back to the first.

parts.description:
A separator between toolbar items or groups.

See https://base-ui.com/react/components/toolbar

inputs.name: orientation
inputs.description: The separator orientation. Defaults to the opposite of the toolbar orientation.
